name: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B
archDisplay: Hybrid-attention MoE (Qwen3-Next lineage): 40 layers, 10x(3x(Gated DeltaNet -> MoE) -> 1x(Gated Attention -> MoE)); 256 experts, 8 routed + 1 shared activated per token; hidden dim 2048; natively multimodal (Causal LM with built-in vision encoder)
note: First open-weight model of the Qwen3.6 generation; sparse MoE (35B total/3B active) that substantially beats its direct predecessor Qwen3.5-35B-A3B and rivals much larger dense models on agentic coding, per the launch blog. This is the single real model behind the leads' 'Qwen3.6 (~35B, open-weights)' and 'Qwen3.6-35B-A3B' entries, which were duplicates of each other (see excluded).

results.notes: SWE-bench Verified; matches the value on its own HF model card's launch comparison table

verificationNotes: Confirmed real via direct fetch of its own HF model card (huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B, full architecture spec) and the official QwenLM/Qwen3.6 GitHub README changelog, which independently states the same release date (2026-04-16; the blog's own publish-date metadata says 2026-04-15, a 1-day variance). The original leads listed this model twice: once as a generic 'Qwen3.6 (open-weights, ~35B, ~2026-04-15)' (sourced to Wikipedia's List of large language models) and once with the precise name/params. Verified these are the same model -- Wikipedia, ollama.com/library/qwen3.6, and unsloth.ai/docs/models/qwen3.6 all resolve to this exact checkpoint -- and consolidated into this single candidate (see excluded for the duplicate entry). Benchmark results are self-reported, cross-confirmed as internally consistent between two Qwen-authored primary sources (its own HF card and the later Qwen3.6-27B blog's comparison table), but no fully independent (non-Alibaba) benchmark corroboration was found in this pass.
